title: The Sea Keeps Our Names
mood: Atlantic noir · trip-pop · drum & bass · saudade · exile
emotion: Grief that has found its proper place — the presence of the absent, held with dignity.
scene: A family table fully set for Sunday dinner, one chair conspicuously empty. The afternoon light falling on it in a particular way that makes the absence feel sacred rather than painful.
symbols: empty chair, Sunday table, afternoon light, place setting, sacred absence
arc: The table being set — hands, careful, Sunday ritual
The empty chair — light falls on it, deliberate
The gathering — family around it, the absent present

Transitions: slow dissolve between shots, no hard cuts. Score: if native audio generation is available, use atmospheric ambient score. commentary.script:
"The Sea Keeps Our Names."
Chapter 13 of Still We Sail.


This song came from a single question I couldn't stop asking myself: Grief that has found its proper place — the presence of the absent, held with dignity.

I wasn't looking for a clean answer. I was looking for the right way to carry the question.

When I wrote this, I kept coming back to certain images. empty chair, Sunday table, afternoon light, place setting, sacred absence. Not because they're poetic — because they're honest. These are the images that carry weight without trying to.

If this song reaches you at the right moment — I hope it gives you permission.
Permission to ask. To sit with not knowing. To feel the weight without rushing to put it down.

That's all any of this is for.
